title: Type Hierarchy Corpus Entry
description: Per-package class / type hierarchy mined from real package metadata. One file per (package, version).

ecosystem.description: Package ecosystem. Lowercase, no version suffix.

package.description: Canonical package identifier within the ecosystem (e.g. 'Microsoft.Data.SqlClient' for NuGet, 'requests' for PyPI, 'gin-gonic/gin' for Go).

version.description: Specific version string the corpus entry was mined from (e.g. '5.2.0'). Different versions live in different files.

license.description: SPDX identifier of the upstream package's license. Corpus entries inherit this license.

harvested_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of when this entry was generated.

harvester.description: Name and version of the harvester tool, e.g. 'csharp/v0.1.0'.

types.description: Map of fully-qualified type name to its hierarchy record. Keys MUST be fully qualified (no short names). Use the ecosystem's native FQN convention (`a.b.C` for Java/Python/C#, `a/b/C` for Go module paths, `a::b::C` for Rust).

kind.description: Native type kind in the source language.

parents.description: Direct superclasses or supertypes, fully qualified. Empty array if no parent (top of hierarchy or interface root). Keep this list small — transitive closure is computed by consumers.

interfaces.description: Implemented interfaces / traits / protocols, fully qualified. Java/C# only — collapsed into `parents` for languages with single-supertype semantics.

type_parameters.description: Generic type-parameter names declared on this type, e.g. ['T', 'K', 'V'] for `Map<K,V>`. Empty for non-generic types.

methods.description: Public methods defined on this type. Optional — many corpus entries record hierarchy only and omit methods. When present, used by SAST tools to know that e.g. `SqlCommand.ExecuteReader` returns a `SqlDataReader`.

deprecated.description: True if the type is marked deprecated/obsolete in upstream. Allows consumers to deprioritise rules targeting it.

returns.description: Return type, fully qualified. Use 'void' / 'unit' / 'None' per language convention; 'object' for fully erased.

params.description: Parameter types in declaration order. Names are best-effort (some metadata sources erase them).
